title: Tillandsia brachypodia
taxonomy: Tillandsia brachypodia belongs to the family Bromeliaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as air plants or tillandsias. This family includes over 3,000 species, many of which are epiphytes, meaning they grow on other plants for support. Tillandsia brachypodia is a specific species within this family, characterized by its unique appearance and growth habits. The genus Tillandsia itself is a group of epiphytic plants native to the Americas, with over 650 species identified. Tillandsia brachypodia's classification reflects its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants in the Bromeliaceae family.

The plant is found in the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The extracts of Tillandsia brachypodia have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms and may have applications in the development of natural antimicrobial agents.
